Connect with Dr. Amanatullah

From Idea to Impact

Most surgical innovation is driven by the fear-of-missing-out, the hype cycle, and manufactured urgency. Hundreds of ideas become a handful of inventions; a handful of inventions become a viable innovation platform. I convert the flaws and fallacies of surgical thinking into foundational technologies for translational products that make a difference in surgery.

Accolades & Affiliations

Zooly Labs

Founded 2025 · zoolylabs.com
Physical AI Disposable Robotics Sensor-Mediated ORS 2026 Business Innovation
“Physical AI that optimizes joint surgery through sensor-mediated disposable robotics, at a fraction of the cost.”

Robotic arms cost millions, sit idle between cases, and lock hospitals into single-vendor ecosystems. Zooly replaces them with disposable sensors that determine motion, impingement, and component orientation in real time, making precision surgery accessible to every operating room at a fraction of the cost.

Intellectual Property

  • US 2023/0293308 — Hip balancer: sensor-mediated component orientation and impingement prediction
  • Pending — Disposable robotic platform for hip, shoulder, and knee (nonprovisional filed 2024)
  • New IDF — Additional invention disclosures filed March 2025

Knimble Designs

Founded 2022
Smart Instruments Knee Arthroplasty Automatic Balancing
“Simplify knee replacement with automatic balancing, linked cutting, and dwell point prediction, without the cost and complexity of robotics.”

Achieving a balanced knee in surgery does not require an expensive robotic platform that most operating rooms cannot even afford. Knimble Designs solves this with smart instruments that automatically balance the flexion gap, link cuts, and predict the dwell point, giving every surgeon a kinematically superior knee, in less steps, and without the robot.

Intellectual Property

  • US 12,023,104 — Dynamic ligament tension-based intraoperative guidance for knee arthroplasty
  • US 11,786,262 — Joint distractor and early disclosure of soft tissue balancing
  • US 11,849,960 — Joint distractor (continuation)
  • Pending — 3-DOF tibial positioning system (US 2024/0423814); staged guide for TKA; intra-articular auto-balancing mini-robot
  • International — PCT, European, Japanese, and Canadian filings for balancing platform

nSight Surgical

Founded 2020 · StartX 2021 · nsightsurgical.com
AI & Computer Vision Operating Room Safety SXSW Top 5 AI HLTH Surgical Transformation
“Like your keys falling out of your pocket, it is easier to find them right then, rather than going back an hour later.”

Surgical teams lose sponges, instruments, and needles inside patients, and contamination events go unnoticed until infection sets in. nSight Surgical uses AI and computer vision to watch the surgery and, in real time, catch what human memory misses before the wound is closed.

Milestones & IP

  • StartX 2021 — Accepted into Stanford’s accelerator program
  • SXSW 2023 — Named Top 5 AI Company
  • HLTH 2023 — Surgical Transformation Award
  • IP — Computer vision for operative safety and foreign body detection (Stanford OTL licensed)

PlantarTech

Founded 2016 · plantartech.com
Dynamic Therapy Patient Compliance Plantar Fasciitis
“Dynamic stretching that patients actually use, increasing compliance and results for one of the most common causes of heel pain.”

Night splints for plantar fasciitis are bulky, uncomfortable, and end up in the closet. PlantarTech replaces static bracing with dynamic stretching that patients actually wear, because a device that sits unused treats nothing.

Intellectual Property

  • Issued — Dynamic tension plantar fasciitis splint (utility patent, 2016)
  • Issued — Splint design patent (2017)
  • Issued — Updated splint design patent (2025)
  • Related — Dynamic hallux tension device disclosure
⚖

Arthrology Consulting

Founded 2016
Device Design Device Consulting Expert Witness
“Most device companies build what engineers imagine. I help them build what surgeons will actually use.”

The gap between what engineers design and what surgeons need in the operating room costs the industry billions in failed products and missed opportunities. Arthrology bridges that gap by embedding firsthand surgical expertise into every phase of device development, from concept through patent strategy, regulatory positioning, and expert testimony when IP or patient safety is at stake.

Industry Partners

Patent Portfolio

  • Revision Cones — 4 issued patents (US 10,182,830 + continuations) for revision knee and hip implant fixation
  • AR in the Operating Room — 3 issued patents (US 10,194,990; US 10,813,700; US 10,806,518) for augmented reality surgical guidance
  • Augment System — 2 issued (2024-2025) for modular bone augments
  • Hip Liners — 1 issued (2024) for constrained liner technology
  • Cool-Cut Saw — 1 issued (2016) for thermal-safe cast removal
  • Design Patents — Repeating “A” brand mark (2020)
  • Provisional — Phase-changing femoral head with variable anteversion (2025)
